lede: A look at July 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Gustav Heinemann and Jarkko Nieminen, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: One Direction
events.desc: The English-Irish boy band One Direction were formed while auditioning for the 2010 series of the British singing competition The X Factor.

events.title: Megawati Sukarnoputri
events.desc: Megawati Sukarnoputri was sworn in as the first female president of Indonesia following her predecessor's impeachment.

events.title: Comet Hale–Bopp
events.desc: Comet Hale–Bopp is discovered; it becomes visible to the naked eye on Earth nearly a year later.

events.title: Glenville shootout
events.desc: Glenville shootout: In Cleveland, Ohio, a violent shootout between a Black Militant organization and the Cleveland Police Department occurs. During the shootout, a riot begins and lasts for five days.

events.title: Philippe Pétain
events.desc: The post-war legal processes against Philippe Pétain begin.

events.title: Bulgaria
events.desc: Bulgarian poet and Communist leader Nikola Vaptsarov is executed by firing squad.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German offensives Operation Edelweiss and Operation Braunschweig begin on the Eastern Front.

events.title: Catalonia
events.desc: In Catalonia, Spain, the Unified Socialist Party of Catalonia is founded through the merger of Socialist and Communist parties.

events.title: Canada
events.desc: Pressed by expanding immigration, Canada closes its doors to paupers and criminals.

events.title: Knights Hospitaller
events.desc: A Knights Hospitaller fleet scores a crushing victory over an Aydinid fleet off Chios.

births.name: Gustav Heinemann
births.line: German lawyer and politician, 3rd President of West Germany

deaths.text: Philippe Pétain, French general and politician, 119th Prime Minister of France
D. W. Griffith, American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Ulysses S. Grant, American general and politician, 18th President of the United States

observances.text: Christian feast day: Liborius of Le Mans
Christian feast day: Mercè Prat i Prat
